pr.title: PR28: Add customer-pack visual tokens and print/mobile layout foundation
pr.body:
This PR starts the first restrained visual layer for the evidence-driven customer pack: calm, print-safe presentation and clearer hierarchy, without introducing diagrams, animations, simulator graphics, or any evidence-copy changes. It focuses on reusable tokens and layout primitives so PDF and portal/mobile surfaces share the same baseline language. - **Visual token foundation (shared, low-ink)** - Introduces pack-level CSS tokens for ink/surface/border palette, spacing rhythm, typography scale, radii, and line-height. - Anchors renderer semantics with explicit hooks on the root document: - `data-visual-tokens="customer-pack-v1"` - `data-layout-mode="print-and-portal"` - **Section rhythm + card hierarchy** - Normalizes section cadence via a consistent spacing rhythm across cover, recommendation evidence, and canonical sections. - Strengthens structural hierarchy with subtle section header separation and card-surface contrast (still print-safe and restrained). - **Confidence badges, warning chips, metric tiles** - Refines badge/chip treatments to severity/confidence variants using muted, print-friendly tones. - Converts metrics into simple responsive tiles (`auto-fit` grid) to improve scanability without adding decorative visuals. - **Print-safe typography + A4 constraints** - Keeps A4 `@page` rules and break-inside controls; tightens print styles to avoid heavy fills and preserve legibility in low-ink output. - **Responsive hooks for portal/mobile compatibility** - Adds explicit layout hook on card grids: `data-layout="responsive-metric-cards"`. - Adds breakpoint behavior for small screens (card header stacking, timeline column collapse, reduced paddings). - **Renderer contract + docs alignment** - Updates renderer assertions/snapshots for new structural hooks. - Updates customer-pack renderer docs to reflect PR28 scope (tokens/rhythm/hierarchy foundation only). ```tsx
...
...
```
pr.author: Copilot
